review_scope: nuclear lamina lattice inside the approved envelope/pore model

score_evidence.notes: Score is manual and scoped to lamina readability. The first lamina render was too sparse and yellow/geometric; the accepted pass increased density, reduced strand thickness, and shifted the visual language toward violet fibers with muted gold accents.

passed_checks: The lamina is positioned inside the nuclear envelope, not outside it.
The lamina is a fibrous irregular network rather than latitude/longitude grid rings.
The network supports the inner membrane visually and remains separate from pores and lumen.
Nucleoplasm, chromatin, nucleolus, cargo, rough ER, and mitosis components were not added in this phase.
minor_issues: The lamina still has some long polygonal spans; a final art pass should add more secondary short fibers.
The color is more readable than biologically literal; final palette should balance violet reference fidelity with gold educational readability.
Roblox export will need curve-to-mesh consolidation and an LOD fallback.
required_fixes_before_final_export: Add LOD variants or a baked texture fallback for the dense lamina network.
Keep lamina as a separate highlightable export group for teaching interactions.
Recheck lamina visibility after adding nucleoplasm transparency.
decision_notes: Phase 02L passes as a scoped lamina foundation and authorizes only the next gated scope: nucleoplasm volume. It does not authorize chromatin or nucleolus yet.
